channel.title: Microsoft Learn - in Intune
channel.description: The latest updates from Microsoft Learn in Intune

item.title: Create a policy using settings catalog in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use settings catalog in Microsoft Intune to configure thousands of settings for Windows 10/11, iOS/iPadOS, macOS, and Android client devices, including Microsoft Office apps, Microsoft Edge, administrative templates (ADMX), and more. Add these settings in a device configuration profile to secure devices, and control different programs and features. Use Microsoft Copilot to get What If analysis, and learn more about each setting.

item.title: Understand Microsoft Intune Management Extension - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Understand Microsoft Intune management extension for Windows.

item.title: Use role-based access control (RBAC) and scope tags for distributed IT - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use role-based access control (RBAC) and scope tags to filter configuration profiles to specific roles.

item.title: Remote Device Actions - Wipe, Lock, Locate, and More - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Discover how to use Microsoft Intune to remotely manage, wipe, lock, restart, and secure Android, iOS/iPadOS, macOS, Windows, and ChromeOS devices. Learn about available remote actions, prerequisites, and bulk actions for IT admins.

item.title: Create a custom role in Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to create a custom role in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Use OEMConfig on Android Enterprise devices in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use Microsoft Intune to manage and use devices running Android Enterprise with OEMConfig. See all the steps, including an overview, see the prerequisites, create the configuration profile in Intune, and see a list of supported OEMConfig apps.

item.title: Device Query for Multiple Devices in Advanced Analytics - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use device query for multiple devices in Microsoft Intune to run Kusto Query Language (KQL) queries, analyze device inventory, and gain cross-platform insights.

item.title: Assign a role to an Intune administrator - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to assign a built-in or custom role to a user in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Add an iOS/iPadOS Line-of-Business App to Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about how to add an iOS/iPadOS line-of-business (LOB) app to Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Common tasks and features in the settings catalog - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use the settings catalog in Microsoft Intune to configure common features. You can create a Universal Print policy, configure Microsoft Edge and Google Chrome web browsers, and use built in settings instead of plist files for macOS devices.

item.title: Configure Recovery Lock using the settings catalog - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Add or create settings using the settings catalog to configure Recovery Lock on macOS devices. Use Recovery Lock to help protect your macOS devices against unauthorized reinstallation and wiping. Using Microsoft Intune, you can configure Recovery Lock settings, and deploy these settings to macOS devices in your organization.

item.title: Remote Device Action: Rotate Recovery Lock Passcode - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to rotate the macOS Recovery Lock passcode with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Role-based access control (RBAC) with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how RBAC lets you control who can perform actions and make changes in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Automatically Hide Devices With Cleanup Rules - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Intune's device cleanup rules offer a simple, automated way to ensure that only actively managed devices remain visible in the admin center. Learn more about device cleanup rules and how to configure them.

item.title: In development - Microsoft Intune
item.description: This article describes Microsoft Intune features that are in development.

item.title: Planning guide to move to Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Plan, design, implement, adopt, and move to Microsoft Intune. Get guidance and advice to determine goals, use-case scenarios and requirements, and create rollout and communication plans, support, testing, and validation plans.

item.title: Tutorial - Walkthrough the Microsoft Intune Admin Center - Microsoft Intune
item.description: In this tutorial, you will tour Microsoft Intune in the Microsoft Intune admin center to better understand how to accomplish tasks.

item.title: Manage and secure devices overview - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Get an overview of the concepts and features you should know when managing devices that access organization resources in Microsoft Intune. You can manage new and existing devices, including BYOD personal devices, check health compliance and view reports, configure device features, and secure devices using mobile threat solutions.

item.title: Help Desk Troubleshooting Dashboard - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Help desk staff use the troubleshooting pane to solve users' technical problems.

item.title: Get started with frontline worker (FLW) device management - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to manage frontline worker devices using Android, iOS/iPadOS, and Windows devices in Microsoft Intune. Get guidance on device use and Intune features built for FLW, like Remote Help. Also, learn about Microsoft Entra shared device mode (SDM) for FLW.

item.title: Windows in cloud configuration - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use Windows in cloud configuration to turn Windows 10/11 client devices into cloud-optimized devices with Microsoft Intune. Cloud config focuses on a simplified device experience for internet browsing and using Microsoft 365 apps, including Microsoft Teams.

item.title: What's new in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Find out what's new in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Configure Microsoft Defender for Endpoint with Intune and onboard devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Connect Microsoft Defender for Endpoint to Intune, onboard devices by platform, and configure compliance and Conditional Access policies that use device risk levels to control access to corporate resources.

item.title: Questions with policies and profiles in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Common questions, answers, and scenarios with device policies and profiles in Microsoft Intune. Learn more about profile changes not applying to users or devices, how long it takes for new policies to deploy, which settings apply when there are conflicts, what happens when you delete or remove a profile, and more.

item.title: Manage attack surface reduction settings with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Configure and deploy policies for devices you manage with endpoint security attack surface reduction policy settings in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Upgrade the Microsoft Tunnel Gateway server software - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Understand how Microsoft Tunnel Gateway upgrades to new versions of the tunnel software for Microsoft Intune.

item.title: What Is App Management in Microsoft Intune? - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about client app management capabilities by platform for Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Add and Assign Managed Google Play Apps to Android Enterprise Devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Understand how to synchronize and assign apps to Android Enterprise devices from the Managed Google Play store.

item.title: Add Apps to Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to add apps to Microsoft Intune so you can assign apps to users and devices. Intune supports a wide range of app types.

item.title: Deploy Windows Update Packages in Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to deploy a Windows update package (.msu file) in Intune.

item.title: Monitor App Information and Assignments - Microsoft Intune
item.description: After you assign an app to users or devices, use this information to help you monitor the app's status.

item.title: Microsoft Intune App SDK for iOS Developer Guide - Multi-Identity - Microsoft Intune
item.description: The Microsoft Intune App SDK for iOS lets you incorporate Intune app protection policies (also known as MAM policies) into your native iOS app. Multi-Identity

item.title: Supported Microsoft Intune Apps - Microsoft Intune
item.description: This article provides lists of support partner and Microsoft apps that are commonly used with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Add an Android Line-of-Business App to Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about how to add a Android line-of-business (LOB) app to Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Integrate Microsoft Defender for Endpoint with Intune for Device Compliance - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Integrate Microsoft Defender for Endpoint with Microsoft Intune as a Mobile Threat Defense (MTD) solution to enforce device compliance and prevent security breaches.

item.title: Remote Device Action: Remove Apps and Configuration - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how apps and configurations can be removed temporarily, then restored automatically or manually using the Remove apps and configuration device action with Intune.

item.title: Third-party device compliance partners support in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use a third-party device compliance partner as a source of compliance data for devices you manage with Intune.

item.title: iOS/iPadOS device enrollment guide for Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Enroll iOS and iPadOS devices using user and device enrollment, automated device enrollment (DEP), and Apple Configurator in Microsoft Intune. Decide which enrollment method to use, and get an overview of the administrator and end user tasks to enroll devices.

item.title: Using Remote Help on Windows to Assist Authenticated Users - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use the Remote Help app to provide remote assistance to authenticated users who also run the Remote Help app, and to troubleshoot for frontline workers (FLW).

item.title: Set up Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Step 1 for deploying or setting up Intune. The starting point is to review supported configurations, sign up for the trial, configure the custom domain name, add users and groups to Intune, assign licenses to users, manage roles, grant admin permissions, and set the MDM authority.

item.title: Get started with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: See an overview of the steps to start using Intune. Plan your move and deployment of Intune, determine your licensing needs and any platform requirements, use compliance and Conditional Access, deploy apps, create device configuration profiles, and enroll your devices to be managed. Get more information on mobile application management for BYOD or personal devices.

item.title: Add, Configure, and Protect Apps with Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Add, configure, and protect apps with Intune.

item.title: Configure compliance policies - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Description for configuring compliance policies

item.title: Protection and configuration levels overview in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about the different levels of protection and configuration in Microsoft Intune, including minimum, enhanced, and high levels. Understand the recommended policies for apps, compliance, and device configuration at each level to secure your organization's resources effectively.

item.title: Configure security, email, VPN, and Wi-Fi device configuration profiles - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Step 4 to deploy device configuration profiles as part of the minimum set of policies for your devices using Microsoft Intune. The starting point is to enable the firewall, install AV, scan for malware, install software updates, create a strong PIN policy, and create email, VPN, and Wi-Fi device configuration profiles.

item.title: Step 5 – Enroll devices in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Enroll Android, Android Enterprise, iOS, iPadOS, Linux, macOS, and Windows devices in Intune.

item.title: Network endpoints for US government deployments - Microsoft Intune
item.description: See the list of US government endpoint URLs that Intune needs and requires. Allow the ports, IP addresses, and endpoint URLs in your proxy server configuration.

item.title: Network endpoints for China deployments - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Review China endpoints for Intune.

item.title: Use Multi Admin Approval in Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Configure Multi Admin Approval to protect your tenant against the use of compromised administrative accounts in Intune.

item.title: Deployment guide for Linux device management - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use our platform deployment guide to set up Linux device management in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Manage approved apps for Windows devices with App Control for Business policy and Managed Installers in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use App Control for Business policies and a managed installer to manage which apps are approved to run on Windows devices that you manage with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Overview of Certificate Connector for Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about the unified Certificate Connector for Microsoft Intune, which supports SCEP, PKCS, imported PKCS, and certificate revocation.

item.title: Set up account driven Apple User Enrollment - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Set up account driven Apple User Enrollment for personal devices enrolling in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Manage firewall settings with endpoint security policies in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Configure and deploy policies for devices you manage with endpoint security firewall policy in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Intune Graph API - Reports and Properties - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about Intune reports and properties provided via Graph API.

item.title: Use Shell Scripts on macOS Devices in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Create, assign, monitor, and troubleshoot shell scripts for macOS devices in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Use Graph APIs to Export Intune Reports - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about exporting Intune reports using Graph APIs.

item.title: Manage antivirus settings with endpoint security policies in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Configure and deploy policies and use reports for devices you manage with endpoint security antivirus policy in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Set up user enrollment with Company Portal for iOS - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Set up the profile based Apple User Enrollment option for personal devices enrolling in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Windows Antivirus policy settings for Microsoft Defender Antivirus for Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: See a list of the settings in the Microsoft Defender Antivirus profile for Windows devices. You can configure these settings as part of Endpoint security Antivirus policy in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Intune endpoint security firewall settings - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Endpoint security firewall policy settings for Windows and macOS in Microsoft Intune

item.title: Use reusable groups of settings policies in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Manage groups of settings for Intune profiles as a single object and then add that settings group object to multiple profile instances. Later changes you make to the settings groups automatically apply to each profile that includes the reusable settings group.

item.title: Configure a VPN or Per-App VPN for Android Enterprise Devices in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use an app configuration policy to add or create a VPN or per-app VPN profile for Android Enterprise devices in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Configure endpoint protection on macOS devices with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use Intune to configure macOS devices use the built-in firewall to allow or block specific apps or to use stealth mode, to use Gatekeeper to determine where apps install, and to use FileVault disk encryption.

item.title: Get support in the Microsoft Intune admin center - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Get online and telephone support for Microsoft Intune and other device management cloud services from within the Microsoft Intune admin center.

item.title: Unlicensed admins in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: This article describes how to give unlicensed admins permissions to access Intune.

item.title: Intune endpoint security Attack surface reduction settings - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Details about the settings in the endpoint security Attack surface reduction policies in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: macOS Antivirus policy settings for Microsoft Defender Antivirus for Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: See a list of the settings in the Microsoft Defender Antivirus profile for macOS. This profile is s part of Endpoint security Antivirus policy for macOS in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Intune endpoint security firewall settings for Configuration Manager devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Endpoint security firewall policy settings for tenant attached devices you manage with Configuration Manager. You can configure firewall setting after you configure tenant attach for Configuration Manager.

item.title: Intune endpoint security Endpoint detection and response settings - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Endpoint security Endpoint detection and response policy settings for deprecated profiles in Microsoft Intune

item.title: Antivirus policy settings for Windows Security experience policy for Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Endpoint security Antivirus policy settings for the Windows Security app in Microsoft Intune

item.title: Windows Antivirus policy settings from Microsoft Defender Antivirus for tenant attached devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: See a list of the settings in the Microsoft Defender Antivirus profile for Windows devices managed by Configuration Manager. You can configure these settings as part of Endpoint security Antivirus policy in Microsoft Intune after you configure tenant attach for Configuration Manager.

item.title: Windows Antivirus policy settings from Windows security settings for tenant attached devices in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Review the settings in the *Windows Security experience* profile for tenant attached devices. To use this Endpoint security Antivirus policy, you must first configure tenant attach for Configuration Manager for Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Configure email settings in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Create an email device configuration profile in Microsoft Intune, and deploy this profile to Android device administrator, Android Enterprise, iOS, iPadOS, and Windows devices. Use email profiles to configure common email settings, including a Microsoft Exchange email server. Add authentication methods to connect to corporate email on devices you manage.

item.title: Configure security baseline policies in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Deploy security baselines that establish a default and recommended security postures on Windows devices you manage with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Configure the Microsoft Managed Home Screen App - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to configure the Microsoft Managed Home Screen app.

item.title: Microsoft Intune documentation - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Manage devices, apps, and security with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Enable MDM automatic enrollment for Windows - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Enable Intune automatic enrollment for Windows devices joining or registering with your Microsoft Entra ID.

item.title: App Protection Policies Overview - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how Microsoft Intune app protection policies help protect your company data and prevent data loss.

item.title: Use Intune Suite add-on capabilities - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Microsoft Intune Suite unifies a series of mission-critical advanced endpoint management and security capabilities. The capabilities of the suite are integrated with Microsoft 365 and Microsoft Security across endpoint platforms for both cloud and on-premises co-managed devices.

item.title: Set up automated device enrollment (ADE) for iOS/iPadOS - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to enroll corporate-owned iOS/iPadOS devices into Microsoft Intune with Apple Automated Device Enrollment (ADE).

item.title: Manage Apple Volume-Purchased Apps - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to sync apps you bought in volume through Apple Business Manager with Microsoft Intune. Then manage and track these apps on iOS/iPadOS and macOS devices.

item.title: Connect Intune account to managed Google Play account - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to connect your Intune account to your Managed Google Play account.

item.title: Set the mobile device management authority - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about how to set the mobile device management authority in Microsoft Intune and some key considerations.

item.title: Manage Windows Update Ring Policies - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about Windows Update ring policies for Windows devices, how to create and manage them, and improve update deployment.

item.title: Microsoft Cloud PKI for Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: An overview of the Microsoft Cloud PKI service, available with Microsoft Intune Suite or as an Intune add-on.

item.title: Plan for Remote Help with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about the requirements and capabilities of Remote Help with Microsoft Intune on Windows, macOS, and Android Enterprise.

item.title: Using Azure Virtual Desktop multi-session with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Guidelines for using Azure Virtual Desktop multi-session with Microsoft Intune

item.title: Sign Up for Microsoft Intune Free Trial Setup Guide - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Sign up for a Microsoft Intune free trial and configure your tenant. Learn the setup process, prerequisites, and how to configure domain names. Start evaluating Intune today.

item.title: Assign Apps to Groups in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to assign an Intune app to groups of users or devices using Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Configure Universal Print policy using settings catalog in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use the settings catalog in Microsoft Intune to create a Universal Print policy for Windows 10/11 client devices. The policy automatically installs printers on your managed devices.

item.title: Update Microsoft 365 apps with the settings catalog in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use settings catalog in Microsoft Intune to update Microsoft 365 apps to the latest version, and choose how frequently Office checks for updates. See the device registry keys that are updated when an Intune policy to Office update is applied.

item.title: Endpoint analytics overview - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Discover how Microsoft Intune endpoint analytics provides actionable insights to optimize device performance, improve user experience, and enable proactive IT troubleshooting.

item.title: Deployment guide to manage macOS devices in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn the recommended processes to manage macOS devices in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Deployment guide to manage iOS/iPadOS devices in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: A guide to managing iOS/iPadOS devices in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Licenses available for Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Intune is available with these licenses

item.title: Audit changes and events in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to review audit logs that record Microsoft Intune activities.

item.title: Add groups to organize users and devices for Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Create Microsoft Entra groups to organize users and devices for use with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: ServiceNow integration with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: ServiceNow integration enables help desk staff, who are licensed to use Remote Help, to use ServiceNow to view incidents and details of the tech issue that an employee is facing from the Troubleshooting pane. This article also describes how to configure ServiceNow integration with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Enroll personal devices in Intune with Android Enterprise work profile management - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Set up Intune for personal devices and bring-your-own-device scenarios using Android Enterprise work profile management.
